title: Ordering at a Café
summary: A customer visits a café and orders a coffee and a pastry. The barista explains the menu options and the customer makes his choices, paying by card at the end.

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: Good morning! Welcome to Bean & Brew. What can I get for you today?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Hi there. Could I see the menu, please? I've never been here before.

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: Of course! It's right here on the board above me. We have hot drinks, cold drinks, and a selection of pastries and sandwiches.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Great. I'd like a large flat white, please. Actually, can you make it a medium?

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: Sure, a medium flat white. Would you like any milk alternatives? We have oat, almond, and soy.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: No thanks, regular milk is fine.

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: Perfect. Would you like anything to eat with that?

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: Yes, I'd like one of those croissants. Are they fresh?

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: They are! We bake them every morning. We also have a chocolate one and a cheese and ham one if you prefer something different.

body.speaker: Customer
body.text: I'll go with the plain one, thank you. How much is that altogether?

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: That's four fifty for the coffee and two twenty for the croissant, so six seventy in total.

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: Absolutely. Just tap your card on the reader here. And your name for the cup?

body.speaker: Barista
body.text: Perfect, James. Your order will be ready in just a moment. Please take a seat!

annotations.meaningVi: cà phê flat white (cà phê espresso với sữa tươi, ít bọt hơn latte)
annotations.exampleEn: She ordered a flat white and a blueberry muffin.

annotations.meaningVi: các loại sữa thay thế (không phải sữa bò)
annotations.exampleEn: Do you offer any milk alternatives, such as oat or almond milk?
I switched to oat milk in my coffee because I'm lactose intolerant.
He had a croissant and a glass of orange juice for breakfast.

annotations.meaningVi: bình thường, không có nhân hoặc topping đặc biệt
annotations.exampleEn: I'll just have a plain bagel, nothing on it.
That comes to fifteen dollars in total.

annotations.meaningVi: chạm thẻ (thanh toán không tiếp xúc)
annotations.exampleEn: Just tap your card on the machine and the payment will go through.
Please hold your card near the card reader to pay.

annotations.meaningVi: ngay lập tức, chỉ một chút thôi
annotations.exampleEn: Your table will be ready in just a moment.

annotations.meaningVi: sự lựa chọn, các món có sẵn để chọn
annotations.exampleEn: The café has a great selection of cakes and sandwiches.

grammarNotes.title: 'I'd like' for polite requests
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I'd like + danh từ/cụm danh từ' là cách lịch sự để yêu cầu hoặc đặt món. Nó lịch sự hơn 'I want'. Ví dụ: 'I'd like a large flat white, please.' — Tôi muốn một ly flat white cỡ lớn. 'I'd like one of those croissants.' — Tôi muốn một chiếc bánh croissant đó. Sau 'I'd like' ta dùng danh từ hoặc 'to + động từ nguyên thể': 'I'd like to pay by card.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'I'd like + noun/noun phrase' to make a polite request or order. It is more formal and polite than 'I want'. Examples: 'I'd like a medium flat white, please.' / 'I'd like one of those croissants.' You can also use 'I'd like + to + infinitive': 'I'd like to pay by card.' This structure is essential vocabulary for shopping, dining, and service situations.

grammarNotes.title: Offers with 'Would you like...?'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Would you like + danh từ / to + động từ?' là cách lịch sự để mời ai đó hoặc hỏi họ có muốn gì không. Ví dụ: 'Would you like any milk alternatives?' — Bạn có muốn dùng sữa thay thế không? 'Would you like anything to eat with that?' — Bạn có muốn ăn gì kèm theo không? Đây là cách nói lịch sự hơn 'Do you want...?' và rất hay được dùng trong nhà hàng, quán cà phê hoặc khi mời khách.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Would you like + noun?' or 'Would you like + to + infinitive?' to make polite offers or invitations. This is more formal than 'Do you want...?' Examples: 'Would you like any milk alternatives?' / 'Would you like anything to eat?' The customer replies with 'I'd like...' or 'No thanks, ... is fine.' This pair — 'Would you like...?' / 'I'd like...' — is the core pattern for service interactions.

translationVi:
Nhân viên pha chế: Chào buổi sáng! Chào mừng đến Bean & Brew. Hôm nay tôi có thể lấy gì cho bạn?
Khách hàng: Xin chào. Tôi có thể xem thực đơn không? Tôi chưa đến đây bao giờ.
Nhân viên pha chế: Tất nhiên! Nó ở ngay đây trên bảng phía trên tôi. Chúng tôi có đồ uống nóng, đồ uống lạnh và nhiều loại bánh ngọt và bánh mì kẹp.
Khách hàng: Tuyệt. Tôi muốn một ly flat white cỡ lớn. À, bạn có thể làm cỡ vừa được không?
Nhân viên pha chế: Được chứ, một ly flat white cỡ vừa. Bạn có muốn dùng sữa thay thế không? Chúng tôi có sữa yến mạch, sữa hạnh nhân và sữa đậu nành.
Khách hàng: Không cảm ơn, sữa thường là được rồi.
Nhân viên pha chế: Hoàn hảo. Bạn có muốn ăn gì kèm theo không?
Khách hàng: Có, tôi muốn một chiếc bánh croissant. Nó có tươi không?
Nhân viên pha chế: Có đấy! Chúng tôi nướng mỗi buổi sáng. Chúng tôi cũng có loại chocolate và loại phô mai và giăm bông nếu bạn thích món khác.
Khách hàng: Tôi sẽ lấy loại thường, cảm ơn. Tổng cộng bao nhiêu vậy?
Nhân viên pha chế: Bốn mươi năm mươi cho cà phê và hai mươi lăm cho bánh croissant, tổng cộng là sáu bảy mươi.
Khách hàng: Tôi có thể trả bằng thẻ không?
Nhân viên pha chế: Tất nhiên. Chỉ cần chạm thẻ vào máy đọc thẻ đây. Tên để ghi lên cốc là gì?
Khách hàng: Là James.
Nhân viên pha chế: Hoàn hảo, James. Đơn hàng của bạn sẽ sẵn sàng ngay lập tức. Mời bạn ngồi!

questions.prompt: What is the name of the café?
questions.explanation: The barista says 'Welcome to Bean & Brew.'
questions.hint: The barista announces the name in her welcome.

questions.prompt: Has the customer visited this café before?
options: No, it is his first time
questions.explanation: The customer says 'I've never been here before.'
questions.hint: He asks to see the menu for a specific reason.

questions.prompt: What size coffee does the customer finally order?
questions.explanation: He first asks for large, then changes to medium: 'Actually, can you make it a medium?'
questions.hint: He changes his mind after his first request.

questions.prompt: Which milk alternative does the customer choose?
questions.hint: He declines all the alternatives offered.

questions.prompt: What food does the customer order?
questions.hint: He asks if the item is fresh before ordering.

questions.prompt: When does the café bake its croissants?
questions.hint: The barista reassures the customer about freshness.

questions.prompt: What flavour croissant does the customer choose?
questions.hint: He does not choose any of the special flavours.

questions.prompt: How much does the croissant cost?
questions.hint: The barista gives both prices separately.

questions.prompt: What is the total price of the order?
questions.hint: The barista adds the coffee and croissant prices together.

questions.explanation: The customer asks 'Can I pay by card?' and the barista says 'Absolutely.'
questions.hint: The customer asks a question about payment.
The barista asks for a name for the cup.

questions.prompt: Where is the café menu displayed?
options: On a paper leaflet at the counter
On a board above the barista
On a screen near the door
questions.explanation: 'It's right here on the board above me.'
questions.hint: The barista points to it while explaining.

cloze.template:
Barista: Good morning! Welcome to Bean & Brew. What can I get for you today?
Customer: Hi there. Could I see the {{b1}}, please? I've never been here before.
Barista: Of course! We have hot drinks, cold drinks, and a {{b2}} of pastries and sandwiches.
Customer: I'd like a large flat white, please. Actually, can you make it a {{b3}}?
Barista: Sure. Would you like any milk {{b4}}? We have oat, almond, and soy.
Customer: No thanks, regular {{b5}} is fine.
Barista: Would you like anything to {{b6}} with that?
Customer: Yes, I'd like one of those {{b7}}. Are they fresh?
Barista: They are! We bake them every {{b8}}.
Customer: I'll go with the plain one. How much is that {{b9}}?
Barista: That's four fifty for the coffee and two twenty for the croissant, so six seventy in {{b10}}.
Customer: Can I pay by {{b11}}?
Barista: Absolutely. Just tap your card on the reader here. And your {{b12}} for the cup?
Customer: It's James.

blanks.explanation: 'menu' — a list of food and drink available in a café or restaurant.
'selection' — a range of items to choose from is a selection.
'medium' — the customer changes from large to medium.
'alternatives' — milk alternatives are non-dairy options like oat or almond milk.
'eat' — 'anything to eat' means any food item.
'croissants' — the customer points to the pastries on display.
'morning' — the barista says 'We bake them every morning.'
'altogether' — 'how much is that altogether?' means the total price.
'total' — 'in total' means the combined price of all items.
'card' — paying by card means using a debit or credit card.
'name' — baristas write the customer's name on the cup.

criticalThinkingQuestion: In many cities, people spend a lot of time and money in cafés. Some people think cafés are an important place to relax and socialise, while others think they are too expensive. Do you think cafés are an important part of city life? What makes a good café experience?
